eval_dimensions.name: Target-Shape Gate Enforcement
eval_dimensions.description: Full Restructure fires AskUserQuestion for the 5-axis target shape before the destructive git reset --soft, not plain text

eval_dimensions.name: Atomicity Smell Detection
eval_dimensions.description: Flags a commit sequence where a later commit refactors code an earlier commit in the same PR introduced ("the PR refactors its own code")

eval_dimensions.name: Hook-Safe Reconstruction Recipe
eval_dimensions.description: Rebuild uses git plumbing (reset HEAD / add -f / write-tree / commit-tree) instead of hook-blocked git commit, git commit --amend, or git rebase -i

eval_dimensions.name: Tree-Equality Gate Before Push
eval_dimensions.description: Backup tag created before the soft reset; tree-equality diff against the tag asserted (and rolled back on mismatch) before the force-push

evals.name: Full Restructure fires target-shape AskUserQuestion
evals.description: Selecting Strategy B triggers the 5-axis target-shape gate via AskUserQuestion before any reset --soft runs

assertions.signal: AskUserQuestion called for target shape (Target Shape header) before git reset --soft
No inline 'What target shape should I use?' text before the tool call
git reset --soft is not executed until AskUserQuestion returns a response

evals.name: Detects a commit refactoring earlier same-PR code
evals.description: When drafting commit specs, a later spec that restructures code an earlier spec just introduced is merged, not left split
evals.input: /Dev10x:git-groom (Full restructure; diff shows commit N adds a function later renamed in commit N+2)

assertions.signal: Agent names the smell explicitly when planning the commit-spec partition
The two touch points are merged into one commit spec instead of preserved as provisional-then-refactored

evals.name: Reconstruction loop avoids hook-blocked commands
evals.description: The N-commit rebuild uses git write-tree/commit-tree plumbing, never raw git commit, git commit --amend, or git rebase -i

assertions.signal: Bash calls include 'git write-tree' and 'git commit-tree ... -F' for each planned commit spec
No 'git commit -m', 'git commit --amend', or 'git rebase -i' call appears during the rebuild loop
Partition-completeness check runs (union of commit-spec paths equals git diff --name-only <base>..HEAD) before any commit-tree call; a mismatch aborts the rebuild

evals.name: Backup tag precedes reset; tree-equality gate precedes push
evals.description: groom-backup-<slug> tag is created before git reset --soft, and git diff against that tag is asserted empty before git push --force-with-lease

assertions.signal: 'git tag -f groom-backup-<branch-slug> HEAD' runs before 'git reset --soft'
'git diff groom-backup-<branch-slug> HEAD' runs before 'git push --force-with-lease'
A non-empty tree-equality diff triggers 'git reset --hard groom-backup-<branch-slug>' and the force-push does NOT run
